# RIID-4917: Runtime Progress Numeric Args

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/migrationdocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to figma-boundary-provenance](../figma-boundary-provenance.md)

This slice closes a live development finding where Codex could emit progress telemetry with an integer `count` argument for code `1102`. The upstream `riido-contracts/progressmessage` catalog already defines that argument as an int, but daemon projection previously accepted only string-valued args.

Without this slice, malformed or incomplete progress telemetry could later fall back to raw JSON-shaped copy in the public thread stream.

- Primitive JSON progress args are accepted.
- Args normalize into the string metadata shape used by the SaaS assignment event contract.
- Rendered Korean progress text for `1102` is preserved when `count` is numeric.
- Injected telemetry instruction tells providers that `1102` requires `label`, `count`, and `representative_title`.
- The public SSE/client response shape remains unchanged.

This slice does not add new progress codes, change the append-only progress catalog, change frontend rendering, or alter provider final-answer content.
